rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Futher Responses To Fca Us Llc'S Special Interrogatories To Defendant Clara Ani Bishop, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAR-04-2026. LINE 1. 2-DEFENDANT FCA, US, LLC's Motion To Compel Futher Responses To Fca Us Llc'S Special Interrogatories To Defendant Clara Ani Bishop, Set One. Hearing required to determine if defendant Clara Bishop has responded to this discovery. If she has not, per the court's statement at the January 14, 2026 hearing, her answer will be stricken. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BRITT ET AL VS. ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING AND ENGINEERING, A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 583.210, Et Seq
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAR-04-2026. LINE 3. DEFENDANT HOLMES US' (F/K/A HOLMES STRUCTURES), ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS HOLMES GROUP (US) INC., Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 583.210, Et Seq. Defendant Holmes US's motion to dismiss all claims alleged against it in plaintiffs' first amended complaint is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that plaintiffs allege that Holmes US is the true name for the fictitiously named Doe 80, Doe 80 is sued in the complaint filed October 6, 2022, no new claims were alleged against Doe 80 in the first amended complaint, and US Holmes was not served with a summons or complaint until December 1, 2025. These facts entitle US Holmes to a dismissal of all claims alleged against it in plaintiffs' first amended complaint per CCP 583.210 and 583.250. All of plaintiffs' arguments in their opposition memorandum lack merit. The mandatory three-year period for service begins with filing of the initial complaint. Where as here, an amended complaint does not add any new claims against the defendant, the filing of the amended complaint does not extend the three-year period. Even assuming that the court should have acted more expeditiously in rejecting plaintiffs' initial effort to file an amendment to identify US Holmes as the true name for Doe 80, the court's delay is not a basis to toll or extend the three-year period. Even assuming that the statute of limitations has not run as to one or more claims against US Holmes, that is not a basis to toll or extend the three-year period. All claims alleged against US Holmes and Doe 80 are dismissed without prejudice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DION BREGMAN VS. TESLA, INC., A TEXAS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAR-04-2026. LINE 9. DEFENDANT TESLA, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Defendant Tesla, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted. Tesla has shown that this case is covered by an arbitration agreement between the parties. That agreement is not unconscionable. The opt-out provision eliminates any procedural unconscionability or, at the least, shows that there is only a small amount of procedural unconscionability. Plaintiff has not identified any substantive unconscionability. Plaintiffs' request to arbitrate at JAMS is contrary to the arbitration agreement. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
